DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendant Regents of the University of California's demurrer to fourth, seventh, and eighth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Eunice Neeley is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to fourth cause of action for negligent retention of an unfit employee; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for injunctive relief; and c) overruled as to eighth cause of action for declaratory relief. The negligent retention claim fails to adequately allege sufficient facts to support such a claim. The Regents cannot be directly liable for negligent retention, but can be vicariously liable for its employees' negligent retention per Government Code 815.2. (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School District (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 868). Ms. Neeley fails to allege facts that fit within the scope of the C.A. case. Ms. Neeley did not oppose the demurrer to the injunctive relief claim and it is well-settled that injunctive relief is a remedy, not a stand-alone cause of action. The declaratory relief claim is adequately pled. The claim is not superfluous because it is possible that Ms. Neeley can prevail on that claim even if she was unsuccessful on her FEHA claims.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
